text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to retrieve or send data. Most modern APIs return information in JSON format, which is easy to read and process. However, many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, so you must include it in your requests. Additionally, APIs usually have rate limits, meaning you can only make a certain number of requests in a given time. If you exceed this limit, you may receive an error. Therefore, proper error handling is essential—you should always check responses for status codes like 200 for success or 429 for too many requests. Understanding these concepts helps you build reliable applications.
